column2: Which network device forwards traffic between IP subnets using destination IP addresses?
column4:
Exam Trap

A switch primarily forwards frames within a local network using MAC addresses. Routing is required to move traffic between IP subnets.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.15; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic HighYield Router DeviceSelection Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which device connects multiple wired devices on a LAN and forwards Ethernet frames according to MAC addresses?
column4:
Why

Switching provides local Ethernet connectivity. A router is needed when traffic must cross an IP subnet boundary.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.15; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic HighYield Switch DeviceSelection Source::Messer-v170

column2: A network requires VLAN configuration, traffic prioritization, port mirroring, and remote administration. Should the technician install a managed or unmanaged switch?
column4:
Why

Those requirements depend on administrative and monitoring features provided by managed switches.

Exam Trap

An unmanaged switch is appropriate for simple plug-and-play connectivity, not networks requiring configuration or visibility.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic HighYield Switch ManagedSwitch Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which device bridges wireless clients onto an existing wired network without inherently providing routing?
column4:
Exam Trap

A consumer wireless router may combine an access point, router, switch, and firewall. A standalone access point supplies the wireless bridge function.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic HighYield AccessPoint Wireless DeviceSelection Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which passive component terminates permanent network cable runs and lets technicians patch those runs to switch ports?
column4:
Why

The patch panel organizes fixed cabling while short patch cables provide changeable connections to the switch.

Exam Trap

A patch panel does not forward, route, or filter traffic.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic PatchPanel Cabling DeviceSelection Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which network device applies rules to allow or block traffic at a network boundary?
column4:
Why

Firewalls enforce traffic policy. Depending on their capabilities, they may evaluate transport information, applications, or both.

Exam Trap

A router forwards between networks; routing alone does not describe policy-based traffic filtering.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic HighYield Firewall Security DeviceSelection Source::Messer-v170

column2: An existing switch does not provide PoE, but one ceiling-mounted access point needs power and data over its Ethernet cable. What should the technician add?
column4:
Why

An injector adds power inline without requiring replacement of a non-PoE switch for a single powered device.

Exam Trap

The injector and access point must support compatible PoE standards.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic HighYield PoE Injector Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A new installation must centrally provide Ethernet connectivity and power to several IP cameras. Which device best combines both functions?
column4:
Why

A PoE switch supplies data and power from its switch ports, avoiding a separate injector for every camera.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic PoE PoESwitch DeviceSelection Source::Messer-v170

column2: A powered device requires PoE++, but the switch supports only PoE+. What should the technician provide before deployment?
column3: Provide a power source that supports the device's required PoE++ standard.
column4:
Why

The source standard must meet the powered device's requirement. A lower-power PoE tier cannot satisfy a higher-tier requirement.

Tech Wisdom

Confirm standards compatibility rather than assuming that every port labeled PoE supplies the same capability.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic HighYield PoE Standards Compatibility Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which customer-premises device connects a local network to broadband service delivered over a cable-provider network?
column4:
Exam Trap

This card identifies the cable-service handoff device. Provider speed tiers and DOCSIS versions are separate implementation details.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic CableModem ProviderHandoff DeviceSelection Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which customer-premises device provides broadband connectivity over a telephone-line connection?
column4:
Why

DSL uses telephone-line infrastructure. Detailed speed and distance comparisons belong to internet connection types rather than this hardware-selection card.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic DSL ProviderHandoff DeviceSelection Source::Messer-v170

column2: Fiber service reaches a customer's premises. Which device terminates the provider's optical connection and presents the customer-side network handoff?
column4:
Exam Trap

Use an ONT for the fiber handoff. Cable and DSL services use different customer-premises devices.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic HighYield ONT Fiber ProviderHandoff Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which component provides a device's interface to a network and contains its physical MAC address?
column4:
Why

The NIC implements the network connection and carries the hardware address used for local frame delivery.

Exam Trap

A MAC address is a data-link hardware address; it is not the device's IP address.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Basic HighYield NIC MAC DeviceSelection Source::Messer-v170
